role_title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications

location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays); flexibility to work from anywhere up to 4 weeks per year

level_signals: Expected to present to CXO-level executives and lead all-hands
Comp range $176,000–$220,000 base in San Francisco area, consistent with Staff-level IC at Lyft

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience driving technically complex products end to end, from inception, to launch with significant user adoption, to sunset

Experience in managing (from product perspective) a team of software engineers and scientists, tracking the progress towards a roadmap through appropriate tools (Jira, Linear, Spreadsheets, Clickup etc)
Experience in crafting data visualizations for the automated measurements of KPIs and metrics, starting from raw databases (SQL, Tableau, Looker, Mode, Python visualization packages etc)
Familiarity with crafting and leading presentations to small groups at the CXO executives level and to big teams (100+ ppl) at an all hands like level in medium to large companies
Ease in driving results and operating independently and with little supervision in flat structured organizations where approvals are not needed but results are closely monitored
Strong understanding of agentic systems, context engineering, and multi-agent orchestration
Strong conceptual knowledge of machine learning and AI
Experience in handling third parties as vendors or customers and knowledge of Saas contract practices and software sales processes
Excellent skills in documenting and articulating complex technical systems.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: hands-on coding experience with standard libraries (e.g., PyTorch, Keras, Scikit-learn, XGBoost) is a plus

scope.surface: Agentic AI product and platform; AI-powered marketplace products within the Central Market Management and Applied Intelligence org
scope.business_model: internal platform (B2B internal); AI/ML platforms powering hundreds of applications across Lyft
primary_metrics_inferred: Adoption of AI platform offerings across Lyft
KPIs and metrics of the org reported to leadership
Decision-making efficiency and effectiveness driven by AI tools
cross_functional_partners: Internal platform users and teams across Lyft
core_responsibilities: Define, own and execute the roadmap of the AI platform and AI-powered marketplace products
Define, track and periodically report on metrics and KPIs of the org
Engage teams and users of AI platform inside the company to understand their needs, evangelize current offerings and identify gaps or opportunities
Write detailed product requirements, strategy and planning documents for technical and non-technical audiences
Proactively engage, research and craft proposals for areas of the company where AI platform can drive efficiency and/or increase effectiveness
Present to non technical leadership on the progress of the team quarterly
Oversee and guide build vs buy decisions, grounding them in data and research

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($176K–$220K base) for San Francisco area
Clear product surface and ownership area (Agentic AI platform and marketplace applications)
Specific tools and technologies named throughout, indicating a technically grounded team
Role is central to a high-visibility org responsible for critical data-driven decisions across Lyft
Hybrid schedule clearly defined (Mondays, Wednesdays, Thursdays in office)
red_flags: Hard requirement of 7+ years PM experience combined with strong agentic AI domain knowledge is a narrow combination
Role requires simultaneous comfort with CXO-level executive communication and deep technical AI/ML expertise — high bar
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Posting first seen and last updated dates not available — unclear how fresh the listing is

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from role title 'Staff' and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description as internal platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.work_auth (inferred as open; not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: The JD lists 'Strong conceptual knowledge of machine learning and AI' as an unconditional bullet (treated as hard) but immediately follows with 'hands-on coding experience... is a plus' (treated as soft). The distinction between conceptual knowledge (hard) and hands-on coding (soft) is clear from JD language.
The JD does not specify whether the role manages direct reports or is a pure IC; treated as non-people-manager based on PM context and no explicit mention of direct reports.
